beginnerGuide.title: Super Battle Golf Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the complete match flow, from your opening swing to the final cup.
beginnerGuide.intro: Super Battle Golf combines golf scoring with real-time movement, checkpoints, combat items, environmental hazards, and multiplayer disruption. Winning consistently requires accurate swings, quick route decisions, smart inventory use, and knowing when to attack or ignore other players.

steps.title: Understand the Match Objective
steps.description: Complete each hole by reaching the cup while managing strokes, travel time, hazards, and attacks from other players. Solo scoring rewards efficient golf, while multiplayer battles also reward reaching the finish before your opponents.
tips: Check the hole layout before taking your first swing.
Prioritize forward progress instead of fighting every nearby player.
Use the safest route when carrying a strong item or protecting a lead.

steps.title: Aim and Control Swing Power
steps.description: Line up the shot direction, judge the expected flight path, and set enough power to reach a safe landing area. Maximum power is useful on open fairways but becomes risky near cliffs, narrow platforms, hazards, and the cup.
tips: Use controlled shots when the landing area is small.
Aim for flat ground to make the next swing easier.
Reduce power near the hole instead of forcing a perfect finish.

steps.title: Move Quickly Between Shots
steps.description: After the ball lands, move toward the next playable position while watching for incoming attacks and environmental hazards. Good movement reduces downtime and helps you secure useful items before other players.
tips: Keep moving when opponents are aiming offensive items.
Avoid standing in crowded item pickup areas.
Approach the ball from the direction of your planned next shot.

steps.title: Activate Checkpoints
steps.description: Checkpoints protect your progress by giving you a safer recovery position after a knockout, fall, or failed shortcut. Skipping one can save time, but it can also send you much farther back if the route fails.
tips: Touch nearby checkpoints during your first attempts on a course.
Skip a checkpoint only when the shortcut has a reliable landing.
Secure a checkpoint before entering a heavily contested section.

steps.title: Manage Your Item Inventory
steps.description: Collect offensive, defensive, and mobility items placed around the course. Inventory space is limited, so keep items that match the next section rather than holding every pickup for the entire hole.
tips: Save mobility tools for climbs, gaps, or major shortcuts.
Keep defensive items when approaching the cup with a lead.
Use weaker disruption items early instead of blocking a better pickup.

steps.title: Use Items at the Right Moment
steps.description: Items are most effective at narrow passages, difficult jumps, checkpoints, and final approaches. A well-timed attack can cost an opponent more time than constant low-value fighting on an open fairway.
tips: Target players during their swing setup or landing recovery.
Place area attacks where opponents must pass.
Do not stop for a fight when you already have a clear route to the cup.

steps.title: Recover from Knockouts
steps.description: Explosions, direct attacks, falls, and course hazards can knock you out and return you to a recent recovery point. Immediately reassess the route instead of repeating the same risky shortcut without the required item or angle.
tips: Move away from the checkpoint after respawning to avoid repeated attacks.
Choose the normal route after two failed shortcut attempts.
Use cover and terrain elevation to block direct lines of fire.

steps.title: Finish the Hole Safely
steps.description: The area around the cup is often the most dangerous part of a multiplayer match. Use a controlled final shot, keep a defensive item ready, and avoid overshooting while opponents create explosions, smoke, lightning, or other disruptions.
tips: Take a short setup shot when the cup angle is poor.
Clear nearby opponents before beginning a slow precision swing.
Use terrain or smoke to hide your final approach.

steps.title: Follow the Beginner Priority Order
steps.description: Your main priorities should be reaching checkpoints, maintaining forward momentum, collecting one useful item, and completing the hole. Combat becomes valuable only when it directly protects your route or delays a close opponent.
tips: Progress first, items second, combat third.
Learn the standard route before attempting advanced shortcuts.
Watch successful players to identify safer landing zones.

quickTips: Check the hole layout and plan a safe landing before your first swing.
Touch checkpoints early so a failed shortcut never erases a large lead.
Carry one mobility item and one defensive item for the final approach.
Attack only the players directly competing for your finishing position.

itemsTierList.title: Super Battle Golf Item Tier List
itemsTierList.subtitle: Compare offensive, defensive, mobility, and disruption items by consistency and match impact.
itemsTierList.intro: The strongest items either create dependable knockouts, deny an important route, or protect progress near the hole. Precision weapons dominate open sightlines, while smoke, weather effects, and close-range disruption become stronger on narrow or crowded courses.

tiers.label: Match-Changing
tiers.description: Dependable items that can create a major lead, stop several opponents, or control the final section of a hole.

entries.name: Railgun
entries.strengths: Fast, precise long-range pressure with strong value on open fairways and narrow bridges.
entries.bestUse: Aim at stationary players preparing a swing, opponents crossing exposed sections, or leaders approaching the cup.
entries.weakness: Requires accurate aim and loses value when terrain, smoke, or tight corners block the firing line.

entries.name: Orbital Laser
entries.strengths: Creates a powerful targeted danger zone that can punish grouped players and block important routes.
entries.bestUse: Place it on checkpoints, narrow paths, crowded greens, or an opponent's expected landing area.
entries.weakness: The warning and delayed impact give alert players time to escape.

tiers.label: Highly Effective
tiers.description: Strong and flexible items that consistently disrupt opponents but require good timing or favorable terrain.

entries.name: Thunderstorm
entries.strengths: Controls a broad section of the course and pressures several players without requiring a perfect direct hit.
entries.bestUse: Deploy it over crowded checkpoints, exposed fairways, or the final green to force opponents to move.
entries.weakness: Less effective when players can quickly leave the affected area or take an alternate route.

entries.name: Smoke Bomb
entries.strengths: Blocks visibility, disrupts precision attacks, and makes it harder for opponents to line up important swings.
entries.bestUse: Cover your final shot, cross an exposed route, escape a Railgun user, or hide a dangerous landing zone.
entries.weakness: Smoke can also obstruct your own aim and does not physically stop opponents from advancing.

entries.name: Launch Items
entries.strengths: Direct knockback can remove opponents from platforms, interrupt swings, or create emergency movement opportunities.
entries.bestUse: Attack players beside cliffs, water, narrow ledges, or the cup.
entries.weakness: Direct-impact tools are easier to avoid on open terrain and may provide limited value without a nearby hazard.

tiers.label: Situational
tiers.description: Useful items with strong specific applications but less consistent value across every map and match state.

entries.name: Flash Camera
entries.strengths: Briefly interferes with nearby opponents and can break their concentration during precision swings.
entries.bestUse: Activate it in crowded tunnels, near the cup, or immediately before making your own final shot.
entries.weakness: Its value drops at long range and against players who are already moving away.

entries.name: Jumbo Burger
entries.strengths: Provides temporary physical presence that is useful for surviving crowded encounters and contesting space.
entries.bestUse: Use it before entering a chaotic checkpoint, narrow passage, or heavily contested green.
entries.weakness: Its defensive value is lower when speed, precision, or a long-range attack is more important.

mapsShortcuts.title: Super Battle Golf Maps and Shortcuts
mapsShortcuts.subtitle: Learn how to approach checkpoints, elevation changes, hazards, narrow paths, and final greens.
mapsShortcuts.intro: Fast course completion depends on choosing landing areas that make the next swing easy. The best shortcut is not always the shortest visible path: a route must also provide a consistent landing, preserve checkpoint progress, and avoid exposing you to easy knockouts.

routes.name: Opening Tee and Long Fairway
hazards: Players landing in the same zone
routes.recommendedRoute: Take a powerful opening swing toward a wide, flat landing area that gives you a clear second shot.
routes.shortcut: Aim past the first safe landing zone only when the fairway remains wide and the next checkpoint can still be reached.

routes.name: Checkpoint Route
routes.recommendedRoute: Approach from the side, activate the checkpoint, and immediately move away from the crowded center.
routes.shortcut: A checkpoint can be bypassed when the following landing area is reliable and failure will not erase a large amount of progress.

routes.name: Climb and High-Ground Section
routes.recommendedRoute: Use several controlled swings to secure flat platforms instead of repeatedly attempting one maximum-power climb.
routes.shortcut: Mobility-assisted jumps can skip switchbacks or reach an upper platform directly.

routes.name: Water and Out-of-Bounds Section
routes.recommendedRoute: Use medium-power shots and land near the center of each safe platform.
routes.shortcut: Skip intermediate platforms only when the destination is wide enough to absorb an imperfect landing.

routes.name: Bridge, Tunnel, and Narrow Passage
routes.recommendedRoute: Wait for the largest attack effects to clear, then move through quickly without stopping for unnecessary fights.
routes.shortcut: Use an outside ledge or mobility route only when it reconnects beyond the choke point.

routes.name: Moving Hazard Section
routes.recommendedRoute: Prepare the swing before the safe opening appears, then release as soon as the path becomes clear.
routes.shortcut: A high or angled swing may clear the moving obstacle instead of waiting for the normal opening.

routes.name: Downhill and Drop Section
routes.recommendedRoute: Reduce swing power and aim for a flat lower platform rather than the farthest visible point.
routes.shortcut: A long drop can bypass multiple turns when the landing zone is wide and protected from out-of-bounds areas.

routes.name: Final Green and Cup
routes.recommendedRoute: Take a controlled setup shot, keep a defensive item ready, and finish from a stable angle.
routes.shortcut: A direct aggressive shot saves time only when the cup has a clear approach and the green is not covered by active attacks.

multiplayerGuide.title: Super Battle Golf Multiplayer Guide
multiplayerGuide.subtitle: Create public or private matches, invite Steam friends, configure lobby rules, and choose between solo, free-for-all, and team play.
multiplayerGuide.intro: Super Battle Golf supports online lobbies for competitive and casual matches. Hosts control lobby privacy, passwords, available player slots, course settings, team rules, and communication options before starting the game.

steps.title: Choose Public or Private Play
steps.description: Use a public lobby to play with available online players or create a private lobby when organizing a match with friends.
points: Public lobbies appear in the server browser.
Private lobbies restrict access through invitations or a lobby password.
The host menu displays the current player-slot limit for the server.

steps.title: Create a Lobby
steps.description: Select the host option, choose the lobby visibility, configure the match rules, and wait in the lobby until the group is ready.
points: Select the course or course rotation.
Choose the game format.
Set the available player slots.
Review team and scoring settings before starting.

steps.title: Invite Steam Friends
steps.description: Open the Steam friends interface while the lobby is active and send invitations to the players you want to join.
points: The invited player must be online in Steam.
Keep the lobby open until all invited players connect.
Friends can also join through the Steam friend menu when lobby permissions allow it.

steps.title: Join with a Lobby Password
steps.description: Players joining a protected lobby must enter the exact password created by the host.
points: Passwords are case-sensitive when the lobby interface treats uppercase and lowercase characters separately.
Share the password privately rather than posting it in a public chat.
Replace the password when unwanted players repeatedly attempt to join.

steps.title: Configure Voice Chat
steps.description: Review the in-game voice settings before the match and select the correct microphone and output device.
points: Adjust microphone volume and voice playback separately.
Use push-to-talk when background noise is a problem.
Mute individual players through the lobby or player interface when needed.

steps.title: Select a Match Format
steps.description: Choose the format that matches the group's preferred level of competition and cooperation.

modes.name: Solo Scoring
modes.description: Players focus on completing holes efficiently, with performance determined by golf results rather than team cooperation.

modes.name: Free-for-All
modes.description: Every player competes independently while using items and course hazards to delay the rest of the lobby.

modes.name: Team Mode
modes.description: Players are divided into teams and coordinate routes, attacks, defensive items, and finishing order.

steps.title: Set Up a Balanced Friends Match
steps.description: Use familiar courses and simple rules for new players, then add more aggressive items and demanding routes as the group learns the mechanics.
points: Balance team sizes before starting.
Avoid placing every experienced player on the same team.
Use private lobbies for practice and route testing.
Confirm that every player has loaded into the lobby before launching the match.

steps.title: Fix Common Connection Problems
steps.description: Most joining problems can be resolved by checking the lobby state, Steam connection, game version, and host settings.
points: Confirm that the host has not already started the match.
Make sure every player is running the same game version.
Restart Steam and the game when invitations stop responding.
Recreate the lobby if it no longer appears in the server browser.
Check that the password and privacy settings match the host's instructions.
Allow the game through the operating system firewall when connections repeatedly fail.

controlsGuide.title: Super Battle Golf Controls Guide
controlsGuide.subtitle: Learn the main keyboard and controller actions, then improve your drives, approach shots, putts, movement, and close-range club attacks.
controlsGuide.intro: Super Battle Golf combines normal golf mechanics with real-time movement and combat. Good players control shot power carefully, move toward the next checkpoint immediately, save disruptive items for important moments, and use jumps, dives, carts, and club swings to prevent opponents from reaching the hole first.

controls.tip: Start moving as soon as the ball is struck instead of waiting for it to stop.
Check the landing area, nearby hazards, opponents, and the next checkpoint before committing to full power.
Jump over small obstacles and combine it with a dive to maintain speed.

controls.keyboard: Movement modifier or assigned dive key
controls.tip: Dive down slopes, through hazards, or toward a loose ball, but avoid diving past the next checkpoint.

controls.keyboard: Aim with the mouse and hold the swing input
controls.controller: Aim with the right stick and hold the swing trigger
controls.tip: Use the projected direction and surrounding terrain to choose a safe landing area.

controls.keyboard: Hold the swing input and release at the desired power
controls.controller: Hold and release the swing trigger
controls.tip: Full power is useful for open fairways, while partial power is safer near cliffs, walls, water, and the hole.

controls.action: Swing the club at an opponent
controls.keyboard: Use the normal swing input while standing near the opponent
controls.controller: Use the normal swing trigger at close range
controls.tip: Approach from the side or rear so the opponent has less time to dodge.
Aim offensive items before activating them and avoid wasting powerful attacks on players far behind.
Keep mobility or defensive equipment ready while travelling and switch to offensive equipment near the hole.
Discard a weak or situational item when a stronger pickup is available.
Use carts on long open sections, but leave early when the route becomes narrow.
Make small steering corrections because sharp turns can roll or redirect the cart.
Reduce speed before ramps, cliffs, tight corners, and crowded checkpoints.

techniques.name: Safe opening drive
techniques.instructions: Aim toward the widest part of the route and use high power without targeting the absolute edge of the fairway.
techniques.bestFor: Starting areas, long fairways, and chaotic public matches

techniques.name: Checkpoint-first shot
techniques.instructions: Prioritize crossing the next checkpoint over gaining maximum distance. A shorter valid shot is faster than returning for a missed checkpoint.
techniques.bestFor: Courses with gates, narrow paths, or multiple route changes

techniques.name: Bank shot
techniques.instructions: Use angled walls to redirect the ball when a direct route is blocked or too dangerous.
techniques.bestFor: Indoor sections, sharp corners, and protected greens

techniques.name: Recovery shot
techniques.instructions: Use moderate power to return the ball to a stable surface instead of attempting another maximum-distance shot from a bad lie.
techniques.bestFor: Slopes, edges, hazards, and crowded terrain

techniques.name: Straight putt
techniques.instructions: Center the aim on the hole and use the lowest power that can comfortably cover the remaining distance.

techniques.name: Uphill putt
techniques.instructions: Add power gradually because the slope will remove speed before the ball reaches the cup.

techniques.name: Downhill putt
techniques.instructions: Use very low power and allow gravity to carry the ball.
techniques.bestFor: Greens where overshooting leads to a long recovery

techniques.name: Crowded-hole putt
techniques.instructions: Approach from an angle that keeps opponents outside your direct swing line, then putt before entering a prolonged club fight.
techniques.bestFor: Public matches with several players already near the cup

techniques.name: Jump-dive movement
techniques.instructions: Jump while moving forward, then dive near the top of the jump to cover ground quickly and clear low obstacles.
techniques.bestFor: Shortcuts, slopes, and escaping enemy attacks

techniques.name: Ball-to-player transition
techniques.instructions: Begin running toward the expected landing area immediately after releasing the shot.

techniques.name: Side-angle club knockback
techniques.instructions: Stand beside the opponent and swing toward an open edge, hazard, wall, or route away from the hole.
techniques.bestFor: Removing players from checkpoints and greens

techniques.name: Item-and-club combination
techniques.instructions: Use a stun, vision-blocking, displacement, or area-control item before closing the distance for a club strike.
techniques.bestFor: Stopping the current leader near the final hole

requirements: Processor, memory, graphics, and DirectX requirements
Native macOS and Linux editions are not announced

achievements.title: Super Battle Golf Achievements and Cosmetics
achievements.subtitle: Track progression goals, multiplayer challenges, knockout objectives, item tasks, hidden achievements, and cosmetic rewards.
achievements.intro: Super Battle Golf has 50 Steam achievements that cover normal golf progression as well as the game's combat, movement, item, and multiplayer systems. Complete easy course objectives first, then use private matches or coordinated public matches for requirements that depend on specific items, knockouts, unusual shots, or several players.

categories.strategy: Play complete matches instead of leaving after a difficult hole. Focus on passing every checkpoint and safely finishing the course.
categories.reward: Progression-related cosmetics are awarded when attached to the completed objective.

categories.strategy: Use safe shots early, save mobility or defensive items for the final holes, and attack only the players directly competing for first place.
categories.reward: Victory cosmetics are unlocked by the matching achievement when listed.

objectives: Finish holes with strong golf scores
categories.strategy: Learn each checkpoint route, use partial-power approach shots, and avoid maximum-power swings near narrow greens.
categories.reward: Score-based cosmetic reward when attached to the achievement.

objectives: Sink the ball with the opening shot on eligible holes
categories.strategy: Practice the same hole repeatedly, copy a consistent camera angle, and adjust power in small increments rather than changing both aim and power after every attempt.
Fight near cliffs, moving hazards, narrow bridges, and greens. A side-angle club swing creates more useful displacement than hitting directly toward a wall.
categories.reward: Combat-themed cosmetic reward when attached to the objective.

objectives: Knock out players with offensive items
Complete objectives tied to specific weapons
Use area attacks against grouped opponents
categories.strategy: Hold high-impact items until players gather near a checkpoint or hole. Railgun and area-control attacks are easier to land when opponents have limited escape routes.
Use visual disruption near precision shots and activate area effects when multiple players are inside the same checkpoint or green.
categories.reward: Associated item or effect cosmetic when attached to the achievement.

objectives: Use golf carts during a match
categories.strategy: Choose open courses with long roads, control speed before corners, and leave the cart before entering narrow putting areas.

objectives: Pick up and use different items
Interact with newer additions such as Railgun, Smoke Bomb, Thunderstorm, Flash Camera, and Jumbo Burger
categories.strategy: Prioritize unfamiliar pickups instead of repeatedly using the same dependable item. Drop unwanted inventory when a required item appears.
categories.reward: Collection or item-themed cosmetic when attached to the completed objective.

objectives: Perform actions that remain concealed until unlocked
categories.strategy: Explore alternative routes, interact with unusual course objects, test items in different environments, and review the hidden-objective section after completing normal progression.
categories.reward: Secret cosmetic reward when the hidden achievement includes one.

objectives: Complete achievements introduced with content updates
Finish objectives on newly added courses or modes
categories.strategy: Play the newest content first and check whether an objective requires a particular map, item, multiplayer count, or game mode.

objectives: Finish demanding item and knockout challenges
Unlock the full Steam achievement set
categories.strategy: Sort unfinished achievements by map, item, and player-count requirements, then complete several compatible objectives during the same match.
categories.reward: Final achievement and any remaining linked cosmetic unlocks.

updatesPatchNotes.intro: Super Battle Golf updates expand both the golf and combat sides of the game. The most important changes include new offensive and utility items, additional playable content, cosmetic rewards, gameplay balancing, stability work, and the announced expansion to PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Nintendo Switch 2.

fixes: Console-specific technical details are handled separately from the current Steam build

updates.date: Published through the linked Steam update
updates.headline: New Battle Items Expand Match Strategy
balance: Expanded offensive, defensive, disruption, and recovery options
More counterplay around crowded checkpoints and final holes
Additional reasons to manage inventory instead of immediately using every pickup
fixes: Item interaction and gameplay fixes included with the content update

updates.headline: Additional Courses and Match Content
balance: Improvements to demanding or easily exploited sections

updates.headline: Shot, Combat, Item, and Movement Adjustments
balance: Changes intended to reduce unavoidable disruption near the hole
fixes: Corrections for inconsistent hits, physics reactions, and item effects

updates.headline: Smoother Matches and Fewer Gameplay Interruptions

faq.title: Super Battle Golf
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Super Battle Golf before you play.

questions.question: What is Super Battle Golf?
questions.answer: Super Battle Golf is a chaotic multiplayer golf game on Steam where one to eight players race through 63 holes while using weapons, utility items, movement, and club combat to sabotage rivals and reach the cup first.

questions.question: Which platforms is Super Battle Golf available on?
questions.answer: Super Battle Golf is available now on Steam for PC, and native PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Nintendo Switch 2 editions have been announced for Summer 2026.

questions.question: How many players can play Super Battle Golf at once?
questions.answer: Super Battle Golf supports one to eight players in the same match, with solo scoring, free-for-all, and team mode options available across public and private lobbies.

questions.question: Does Super Battle Golf have single-player content?
questions.answer: Yes. Super Battle Golf includes single-player support, so you can practice courses, swing angles, and item timing on your own before joining public or private multiplayer matches.

questions.question: How many courses and holes does Super Battle Golf include?
questions.answer: Super Battle Golf currently features 63 holes across multiple biomes such as Forest, Desert, Coast, Snow, and City, each with checkpoints, hazards, shortcuts, and hole-in-one opportunities.

questions.question: How many achievements does Super Battle Golf have?
questions.answer: Super Battle Golf has 50 Steam achievements covering progression, victories, knockouts, item use, mobility, secret objectives, and cosmetic unlocks.

questions.question: Does Super Battle Golf support controllers?
questions.answer: Yes. Super Battle Golf supports keyboard and mouse as well as controllers, including full swing aiming, item use, movement, and golf cart controls on both input methods.

questions.question: Will Super Battle Golf keep receiving updates?
questions.answer: Yes. Super Battle Golf continues to receive new items, courses, cosmetics, balance changes, and stability fixes through Steam News, and the announced console editions are tracked in the updates guide.
